text.source_text: इत श्चासंसारिस्वरूपप्रतिपादनपरमेवैतद्वाक्यमित्यवगन्तव्यम् ; यदस्मिन्वाक्ये पत्यादयः शब्दा असंसारिस्वरूपप्रतिपादनपराः संसारिस्वभावप्रतिषेधनाश्च भवन्ति — ‘ सर्वस्य वशी सर्वस्येशानः सर्वस्याधिपतिः ’ इत्येवंजातीयका असंसारिस्वभावप्रतिपादनपराः । ‘ स न साधुना कर्मणा भूयान्नो एवासाधुना कनीयान् ’ इत्येवंजातीयकाः संसारिस्वभावप्रतिषेधनाः । तस्मादसंसारी परमेश्वर इहोक्त इत्यवगम्यते ॥ ४३ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: ita ścāsaṃsārisvarūpapratipādanaparamevaitadvākyamityavagantavyam ; yadasminvākye patyādayaḥ śabdā asaṃsārisvarūpapratipādanaparāḥ saṃsārisvabhāvapratiṣedhanāśca bhavanti — ‘ sarvasya vaśī sarvasyeśānaḥ sarvasyādhipatiḥ ’ ityevaṃjātīyakā asaṃsārisvabhāvapratipādanaparāḥ | ‘ sa na sādhunā karmaṇā bhūyānno evāsādhunā kanīyān ’ ityevaṃjātīyakāḥ saṃsārisvabhāvapratiṣedhanāḥ | tasmādasaṃsārī parameśvara ihokta ityavagamyate || 43 ||

translation.literal_translation: And for this reason too this passage should be understood as teaching the non-samsaric nature: in this passage words such as 'lord' and related terms positively teach non-samsaric nature and negate samsaric traits. Expressions like 'controller of all, ruler of all, overlord of all' present the non-samsaric nature. Expressions like 'he is not greater by good action nor smaller by bad action' negate the defining marks of transmigrating existence. Therefore it is understood that the non-samsaric Supreme Lord is what is taught here. (43)
translation.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.3.43 adds patyadi-shabda evidence: sovereignty predicates and karma-transcendence clauses signal Ishvara, not conditioned jiva.
translation.interpretive_note: Lexical sovereignty markers function as decisive determination criteria.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S03 pass with stable cadence and inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit suṣupti/utkranti and patyadi technical term choices in records 1.3.106-1.3.110.
